fieldValues.value: Artists retain ownership of and responsibility for their own work. The project does not insure exhibited work. Artists are informed of this before agreeing to show.
The back stairwell cannot be locked — it is also a fire escape route. There is no practical mechanism to insure or protect work against theft or accidental damage in an open-access space.

Artists must make their own insurance decisions. The project is not in a position to take on liability for others' work and is not a commercial operation. Informing artists upfront keeps the arrangement honest.
